column2: A chronic or acute pathological condition, disorder, or complication that affects the body and is the primary subject of medical diagnosis and treatment.
A pharmacological agent, medication, or chemical substance prescribed or administered to prevent, treat, or manage a disease or clinical condition.
A clinical sign, patient-reported feeling, or physiological finding that indicates the presence or severity of a disease or condition.
A quantifiable laboratory measurement, clinical assessment, or diagnostic test result used to evaluate disease status, treatment response, or health outcomes.
A specific organ, tissue, or structural location in the human body that is associated with or affected by a disease finding or complication.
A non-pharmacological clinical intervention, surgical procedure, or structured therapeutic activity used to manage or treat a disease.
A quantitative measurement of drug dosage, titration percentage, clinical threshold level, or numeric treatment parameter expressed with units (e.g., 20%, <110 mg/dL, 5 units, 0.5 U/kg/day).
